public_reasoning_boundary: This artifact records public hypotheses, structures, unknowns and evidence requirements. It does not claim access to hidden reasoning.

theta.software_topology: software entities, states and proof obligations only

active_basis_coordinates.expansion: Every Cartesian product coordinate W x L x H is active.

interpretations.text: Every deploy, repair, rollback and restore action must preserve one exact release identity and one authorized operator path.
The five-service release unit, employee-network boundaries and Host Provisioner Docker authority must agree across compose, source and runtime checks.
Release construction must bind Git SHA, image digests, configuration hashes, migration head and independently verifiable manifest.
Critical partial destructive effects, crash points, false health, ambiguous outcomes and restart ordering require deterministic failure treatment.
Repair, backup, restore and whole-release rollback must preserve accepted work and refindable proof.
Every terminal or blocked state must be diagnosable, bounded and linked to a safe next action.
WS-09 groundwork measures truthful saturation and fairness for one 64 GiB host and 25-30 employee runtimes; 200-500 is future pressure only.
Release proof, restore proof and saturation state may become reusable trust and support capabilities without promoting acceptance.

reusable_templates.name: authority-preserving state machine
reusable_templates.instantiation: Map actor, release identity, command, durable state, side effect, terminal state and repair permission.

reusable_templates.name: exact-identity chain
reusable_templates.instantiation: Bind Git SHA, five image digests, compose/config hashes, migration head, secret versions and signed manifest.

reusable_templates.name: partial-effect ledger
reusable_templates.instantiation: Enumerate crash before effect, during effect, after acceptance, after durable receipt and before proof projection.

reusable_templates.name: rollback compatibility envelope
reusable_templates.instantiation: Check application, schema, image, config and secret compatibility while preserving accepted work.

reusable_templates.name: restore continuity proof
reusable_templates.instantiation: Bind backup ID to database, filesystem, secret version, restore result and proof refinding.

reusable_templates.name: topology truth table
reusable_templates.instantiation: Require five services, exact health dependencies, scoped employee networks, bounded egress and Host Provisioner-only Docker authority.

reusable_templates.name: operator terminality contract
reusable_templates.instantiation: Every ambiguous, failed, degraded or saturated state names retry permission, blocking reason, evidence and safe next action.

reusable_templates.name: capacity/fairness pressure harness
reusable_templates.instantiation: Measure connection, queue, SSE, CPU, memory and noisy-neighbor effects at the 25-30 runtime target.

hypotheses.statement: Production compose may not encode one canonical five-service release unit with exact health dependencies.
One or more production images may use floating tags or omit source-SHA/digest binding.
Release manifests may be self-asserted rather than independently recomputable.
Rollback may be service-local and fail to preserve whole-release compatibility or accepted work.
Backup/restore may omit filesystem, secret-version or proof-refinding continuity.
Health checks may report success for partial topology or before migration/runtime readiness.
Fault injection may be absent, nondeterministic or destructive without bounded cleanup.
Repair paths may rewrite application projections instead of validating database-authoritative returned records.
Manager or Model Gateway may retain Docker control outside Host Provisioner authority.
Employee network isolation or bounded egress may be declarative but not behaviorally verified.
Secret versions may not be release-bound, redacted in diagnostics or restored coherently.
Telemetry may lack stable command/effect/release lineage across failure and recovery.
Workflows may build workspaces but not all five exact-SHA release images and inspect their digests.
Artifact upload or continue-on-error wrappers may obscure a failed release command.
Capacity harnesses may omit per-employee fairness, queue delay or SSE accounting.
Pilot schemas may omit saturation stop authority, rollback trigger, incident owner or customer exit state.
Temporal authority may mix replica clocks with database clocks for leases, expiry, retry or health.
Ambiguous recovery may permit retry without accepted proof, nonacceptance proof or operator-blocked terminality.
Migration rollback compatibility may be documented but not executable against the exact release unit.
The 25-30 runtime target may exceed current connection, queue, memory or network assumptions.

forbidden_transitions.reason: No health success for a partial release unit.
Accepted work cannot be duplicated during retry, repair, rollback or restore.
Retry requires accepted proof, nonacceptance proof or explicit operator-blocked terminality.
Host Provisioner alone owns Docker authority.
Restore is incomplete until proof is refindable and release identity is verified.
Application validates database-authoritative returned records; it does not rewrite them.
Shared temporal authority must be database-owned where concurrency matters.
Rollback cannot discard accepted durable work.
Groundwork and synthetic pressure do not establish capacity or pilot acceptance.

expected_closure_regressions: Direct Manager composition may have left stale infra callers or workflow assumptions.
Migration 0078 may require release and rollback compatibility updates.
PR #35 exact-head CI may be red or incomplete.
Compose syntax may pass while health ordering or network authority remains incomplete.
Images may build independently without forming one coherent release manifest.
Backups may restore incompletely or fail proof refinding.
Fault injection may strand durable state or containers.
Rollback may restore images without schema/config/secret compatibility.
Capacity probes may hide noisy-neighbor starvation.
PR and active documents may lag executable release/recovery topology.

operator: One bounded command surface for deploy, repair, restore and rollback outcomes.
Explicit blocked states with retry permission and evidence.
Release and restore diagnostics without secret exposure.
product: Refindable restore proof can become a customer-trust capability.
Signed release identity can reduce false-success support incidents.
Saturation state can support truthful degraded behavior.
architecture: Release identity crosses source, images, compose, schema and secrets.
Failure, repair and rollback share stable identities and telemetry lineage.
future_capacity: Connection, queue and SSE metrics become per-employee and fairness-aware.
Noisy-neighbor descriptors guide later multi-host partitioning.
200-500 employees remain pressure inputs, not accepted capacity.

explicit_unknowns.unknown: Exact conclusion of PR #35 head workflow.
explicit_unknowns.evidence_required: GitHub Actions exact-head run and retained logs/artifacts.

explicit_unknowns.unknown: Canonical production compose and five-service release unit.
Image tag, digest and SHA behavior for Manager, Gateway, Provisioner, Web and Caddy.
explicit_unknowns.evidence_required: Dockerfiles, build workflows and image metadata.

explicit_unknowns.unknown: Deployment, smoke, repair, rollback and backup/restore implementations.
explicit_unknowns.evidence_required: All transitive infra callers and tests.

explicit_unknowns.unknown: Whether health is topology-complete and migration-aware.
explicit_unknowns.evidence_required: Health source, compose dependencies and behavioral tests.

explicit_unknowns.unknown: Whether Host Provisioner is the sole Docker authority.
explicit_unknowns.evidence_required: Repository-wide call search and negative tests.

explicit_unknowns.unknown: Whether employee network isolation and bounded egress are executable.
Whether rollback preserves accepted work across schema, image, config and secrets.
Whether backup/restore includes database, filesystem, secret versions and proof refinding.
explicit_unknowns.evidence_required: Backup/restore source, manifests and restore tests.

explicit_unknowns.unknown: Measured resource envelope for 25-30 runtimes on 64 GiB.
Whether pilot stop/rollback schemas exist and are authority-bound.
Availability of signing keys, registry, target Linux host, managed database and backup store.
explicit_unknowns.evidence_required: External environment and credential inventory; never inferred from repository.
